title: Foxglove Records community-radio ad spot: clean the recorded VO takes + station tag, confirm the read against the guide mix, and assemble a mix-ready :30/:60 audio package

source.reference: Clean up and assemble already-recorded voiceover takes into a mix-ready community-radio ad-spot audio package

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: "This is an assembly-and-motion job, not a from-scratch creative project. I'm handing you everything, already done" — including "The voiceover (I record it separately and send you the audio)" plus "a music bed" and the instruction to "assemble it cleanly." REASONABLY REFRAMED to the slot's AUDIO deliverable (a community-radio ad spot) and a fictional, IP-clean brand: the client still hands over everything already recorded — the spoken voiceover takes, a supplied music bed, and a station tag line — and the job is a pure assembly-and-clean pass, NOT generative. The Adobe connector's honest audio surface does the genuinely-doable work: media_enhance_speech splits each supplied voice recording into a clean speech stem (room hiss / HVAC hum / reverb removed) plus its background/reverb stems, and media_summarize reads the client's rough guide mix back so the script/timing cue sheet is confirmed against what was actually recorded. The one step the connector CANNOT do — the final multi-track LAYER-MIX (ducking VO over the music bed, loudness/broadcast mastering, cross-fades) — is stated plainly as the human-finish step (there is no mix/loudness tool in the connector palette; media_enhance_speech is voice-only separation, not mastering). Nothing is invented beyond the ad spot the brief-shaped intent asks for: assemble the supplied parts into a clean, organised, mix-ready package.
one_line_ask: Take the label's already-recorded voiceover takes, station tag, supplied music bed, and rough guide mix, clean every spoken recording to an isolated speech stem, confirm the read against the guide mix, and hand back one organised, mix-ready :30/:60 radio-spot audio package (final layer-mix flagged as the human-finish step).
full_brief:
Foxglove Records, a small independent label that co-runs a volunteer community-radio arts programme, is booking a run of on-air ad spots to promote its spring 'Porch Sessions' live-music series. This is an assembly-and-clean job, not a from-scratch creative project — everything is already recorded and the label is handing it all over. Supplied: (1) a :60 voiceover read of the full spot recorded by a volunteer host on a USB mic in a spare room (warm, conversational, but with steady room hiss, a bit of HVAC hum, and light room reverb); (2) a tighter :30 cut-down read of the same script; (3) a spoken station/legal tag line ('Foxglove Community Radio, 98.3 — supported by listeners like you') recorded in the same room; (4) a supplied instrumental music bed (a gentle acoustic guitar loop the label already cleared for on-air use) as a WAV; and (5) a rough 'guide mix' the host quickly recorded on a phone with the voiceover talking over the music bed, so you can hear the intended pacing. There is also a script + timing cue sheet (CSV) listing each line, its target in/out seconds, and which take it belongs to.

What the label needs back is a clean, organised, mix-ready audio package for the two spot lengths: every spoken recording denoised and de-reverbed down to an isolated, broadcast-clear speech stem so the voice sits clean; the supplied music bed carried through untouched as the bed; the guide mix listened back and summarised so the confirmed script/timing cue sheet matches what was actually read; and all of it gathered into one dated Creative Cloud handoff folder with the cue sheet, so the label's volunteer engineer can drop the stems onto the timeline and do the final layer-mix. IMPORTANT and stated up front: the final multi-track mix itself — ducking the voiceover over the music bed, loudness/broadcast mastering to the station's target level, and the cross-fades — is a manual finish in the engineer's DAW and is out of scope for this automated audio-prep pass; this pass delivers the cleaned speech stems, the confirmed cue sheet, the carried-through bed, and the organised package. Deliverables: cleaned :60 VO speech stem, cleaned :30 VO speech stem, cleaned station-tag speech stem, the confirmed script/timing cue sheet, the supplied music bed carried into the package, and one organised mix-ready Creative Cloud handoff folder.

inputs.name: vo_read_60s.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: A warm, conversational volunteer-host voiceover reading a ~60 second community-radio ad read for an indie label's spring live-music series, e.g. 'This spring, Foxglove Records brings the Porch Sessions back to the neighbourhood — six Saturday evenings of live acoustic music on the front steps of the old library. Free, all ages, bring a folding chair. Doors and porches open at six...'. Single friendly mid-range voice, unhurried. Render to WAV, then deliberately degrade for an audio-repair task: add steady low room hiss, a faint HVAC hum, and light room reverb so it sounds recorded on a USB mic in a spare room.
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally noisy/reverberant (NOT a clean 48k loudnorm broadcast stem) so media_enhance_speech has a genuine denoise / de-hiss / de-reverb job and a real speech-vs-background separation to perform. Voice only, no music in this file.

inputs.name: vo_read_30s.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: A tighter ~30 second cut-down of the same Foxglove Porch Sessions ad read by the same volunteer host, faster and punchier, hitting the series name, that it's free and all-ages, and the Saturday-evening timing. Single friendly mid-range voice. Render to WAV, then degrade with the same room hiss, faint HVAC hum, and light reverb as the :60 read so both takes need the same cleanup and match tonally.
inputs.realism_notes: Second spoken take with the same recorded-in-a-room imperfections so the two VO stems can be cleaned to a consistent broadcast-clear sound; gives the package its :30 length.

inputs.name: station_tag_line.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: A short spoken station/legal tag line, ~6 seconds, same volunteer host voice and same room: 'Foxglove Community Radio, ninety-eight point three — supported by listeners like you.' Render to WAV then apply the same room hiss, HVAC hum, and light reverb as the other takes.
inputs.realism_notes: A separate short spoken recording that also needs the same clean-up pass; kept as its own stem so the engineer can place the tag independently at the tail of both spots.

inputs.name: music_bed_acoustic_loop.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: SUPPLIED CLEARED ASSET (not model-generated for this task): a gentle, unobtrusive acoustic-guitar instrumental loop, roughly 70 seconds, mid-tempo, no vocals, already cleared by the label for on-air use, delivered as a clean WAV to sit under the voiceover as the music bed.
inputs.realism_notes: Carried through the package untouched — the connector audio tools do not compose or master music, so the bed is a supplied instrumental the label already cleared, exactly as the grounding brief hands over 'a music bed'. It is NOT run through media_enhance_speech (that is voice-only separation); it is only stored/copied into the handoff package.

inputs.name: guide_mix_phone_scratch.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: A rough phone-recorded 'guide mix' of the :60 spot: the same host voiceover talking over the acoustic music bed at once, captured on a phone in the room so the two are blended, a little boomy and quiet, meant only to convey intended pacing and which line lands where. Render the spoken read to WAV over a faint bed and degrade to sound phone-recorded (limited bandwidth, room tone, slight clipping).
inputs.realism_notes: A blended voice-plus-music scratch so media_summarize has real spoken narration to read back for confirming the script/timing; provided purely as a pacing reference, not a deliverable.

inputs.name: spot_script_timing_cues.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns line_id,take,line_text,target_in_seconds,target_out_seconds,notes describing the Foxglove Porch Sessions radio spot: rows for each line of the :60 read, the :30 cut-down read, and the station tag, with target in/out seconds within each spot length and short notes like 'duck bed under VO here' or 'tag over bed tail'. Reference the takes by the supplied filenames (vo_read_60s, vo_read_30s, station_tag_line).
inputs.realism_notes: A realistic script + timing cue sheet the engineer uses to place the cleaned stems on the timeline; it is confirmed/annotated against what media_summarize reads back from the guide mix, and referenced by take so the package is self-describing.

outputs.name: vo_read_60s_clean.wav
outputs.spec: Cleaned :60 voiceover speech stem: room hiss, HVAC hum, and reverb suppressed by media_enhance_speech (voice-only isolation), WAV — the broadcast-clear voice for the :60 spot. The tool's separated background/reverb stems are also returned but discarded for delivery.

outputs.name: vo_read_30s_clean.wav
outputs.spec: Cleaned :30 voiceover speech stem, matched in tone to the :60 stem, WAV — the broadcast-clear voice for the :30 cut-down spot.

outputs.name: station_tag_clean.wav
outputs.spec: Cleaned station/legal tag-line speech stem, WAV, ready to place at the tail of either spot.

outputs.name: spot_script_timing_confirmed.csv
outputs.spec: The script/timing cue sheet confirmed against the guide-mix summary: each line, its take, target in/out seconds and mix notes verified to match what was actually read, so the engineer's timeline placement is correct.

outputs.name: music_bed_acoustic_loop.wav
outputs.spec: The supplied instrumental music bed carried into the handoff package untouched (no connector audio processing applied to music), so it sits alongside the cleaned voice stems for the manual mix.

outputs.name: foxglove_spot_package_folder
outputs.spec: One dated Creative Cloud handoff folder gathering the cleaned :60 / :30 VO stems, the cleaned tag stem, the confirmed cue-sheet CSV, and the supplied music bed — the mix-ready audio package the volunteer engineer opens to do the final layer-mix (ducking, loudness mastering, cross-fades are the flagged human-finish step).

connector_workflow.note: Initialize a block upload of the client's :60 voiceover read (vo_read_60s.wav, media_type audio/wav) into the label's Creative Cloud project folder. Returns the transfer document + presigned block URL. First source load.
Finalize the :60 VO upload from step 1 into a real CC audio assetId that media_enhance_speech can consume.

Separate the :60 VO into an isolated clean speech stem (room hiss / HVAC hum / reverb suppressed) plus background/reverb stems; keep the speech stem as the :60 broadcast voice. [A] async-widget; voice-only isolation, not loudness mastering. Consumes step-2 assetId.
Initialize a block upload of the :30 cut-down read (vo_read_30s.wav). Second source load.
Finalize the :30 VO upload from step 4 into a real CC audio assetId.
Clean the :30 read the same way so both VO stems match tonally — isolated speech stem, hiss/hum/reverb removed. [A] async. Consumes step-5 assetId.
Initialize a block upload of the spoken station/legal tag line (station_tag_line.wav). Third source load.
Finalize the tag-line upload from step 7 into a real CC audio assetId.
Clean the short station-tag recording to an isolated speech stem so the legal/station line is crisp at the tail of either spot. [A] async. Consumes step-8 assetId.
Initialize a block upload of the rough phone guide mix (guide_mix_phone_scratch.wav) — VO over the bed — used only to confirm pacing/script. Fourth source load.
Finalize the guide-mix upload from step 10 into a real CC audio assetId that media_summarize can read.
Read back the spoken content of the guide mix (dialogue/narration) so the actual read — line order and pacing — is confirmed. [A] async. Consumes step-11 assetId; feeds the cue-sheet confirmation.

Initialize a block upload of the script/timing cue sheet reconciled against the guide-mix summary (spot_script_timing_confirmed.csv, media_type text/csv) so the confirmed sheet lives with the package. Consumes the step-12 summary as the confirmation basis.
Finalize the confirmed cue-sheet CSV into the client's CC project folder as the timing deliverable the engineer places stems against.

Initialize a block upload of the supplied, already-cleared instrumental music bed (music_bed_acoustic_loop.wav) so it is carried into the package untouched. Fifth source load — the bed is stored, never audio-processed by the connector.
Finalize the music-bed upload from step 15 into a real CC audio assetId that sits alongside the cleaned voice stems for the manual mix.
Create a dated handoff folder in the label's Creative Cloud (e.g. /Foxglove/PorchSessions_Spot_v1) to gather the mix-ready package into one place.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: vo_read_60s_clean.wav, vo_read_30s_clean.wav, station_tag_clean.wav, spot_script_timing_confirmed.csv, music_bed_asset, spot_package_folder
connector_workflow.note: Copy the deliverables — cleaned :60 stem (3), cleaned :30 stem (6), cleaned tag stem (9), confirmed cue-sheet CSV (14), and the carried-through music bed (16) — into the dated folder from step 17, producing the single organised mix-ready package the volunteer engineer opens. The final layer-mix (VO ducking over the bed, loudness/broadcast mastering, cross-fades) is the flagged human-finish step — no connector audio-mix/mastering tool exists.

chaining_note: One continuous output-to-input chain over five supplied audio assets plus a cue sheet, converging on a single packaged folder. Each spoken recording follows the same init->finalize->clean idiom: vo_read_60s uploads (1) -> finalizes (2) -> media_enhance_speech = vo_read_60s_clean (3); vo_read_30s uploads (4) -> finalizes (5) -> clean (6); station_tag uploads (7) -> finalizes (8) -> clean (9). The guide mix uploads (10) -> finalizes (11) -> media_summarize = guide_mix_summary (12), which feeds the cue-sheet confirmation: the confirmed CSV uploads (13, inputs_from the summary) -> finalizes (14). The supplied music bed uploads (15) -> finalizes (16) and is carried untouched. A dated handoff folder is created (17), then asset_copy_assets (18) consumes the three cleaned stems (3/6/9), the confirmed cue sheet (14), and the music-bed asset (16) plus the folder (17) to produce the single mix-ready package. Every post-init step sets inputs_from to a prior step's named output (each init is immediately consumed by its finalize, which is chained); the deliverable package emerges wholly from the chain, with no free-standing step.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon audio-CREATE task: 18 connector calls across 2 groups (asset/storage [C] and the [A] async audio media tools), 6 distinct Adobe tools — at/above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor. It is hard for the honest reason audio work is hard on this surface: the agent must recognise that a radio-spot assembly maps to (a) three separate voice-cleanup passes via media_enhance_speech (voice-only stem isolation, run per take so the stems match), (b) a media_summarize read-back of the rough guide mix to CONFIRM the script/timing rather than invent it, and (c) carrying the supplied, cleared music bed through UNTOUCHED because the connector has no music-processing tool — then gathering everything into one organised CC handoff folder. Crucially it re-scopes the brief's 'assemble it cleanly' honestly: the final multi-track LAYER-MIX (ducking VO over bed, loudness/broadcast mastering, cross-fades) has NO connector tool and is explicitly named as the single human-finish step. It respects every hard limit: no generative audio, no text-to-audio / voice synthesis of new lines, no music generation or mastering, media_enhance_speech is used only for voice isolation, media_summarize only reads existing narration, and no banned image/video capability appears. The three media steps are correctly [A] async-widget; the rest are headless [C] asset ops.

reverify.banned_capability_check: PASS — none of the banned capabilities are used. There is no image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop, no generative fill, no text-to-image or text-to-audio / from-scratch synthesis, no upscaling / super-resolution, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no bespoke font/letterform design, and no unreachable external account. The only content-producing audio tool used, media_enhance_speech, is separation/denoise of SUPPLIED recordings (it isolates a clean speech stem from voice already recorded by the client) — it does not synthesize or generate new audio. media_summarize only reads back existing spoken narration. The music bed and all voice takes are client-supplied inputs.
reverify.notes: Every potentially-risky step is in-bounds: (1) media_enhance_speech (n3,n6,n9) is voice-only stem isolation on the client's own recordings, not music mastering or loudness — matching its documented behaviour (splits a file into cleaned speech / background / reverb); the discarded background+reverb stems are noted. (2) media_summarize (n12) runs on the guide mix, which by construction contains real spoken narration (the host reading over the bed), satisfying the tool's dialogue/narration requirement. (3) The music bed is a SUPPLIED, already-cleared instrumental carried through untouched (n15/n16 store it; it is never sent to any audio-processing tool) — the connector cannot and does not generate or master music. (4) The final multi-track layer-mix / ducking / loudness mastering / cross-fades has no connector tool and is explicitly flagged as the human-finish step in the brief, outputs, and difficulty_rationale, so no capability is over-claimed. (5) All 18 steps are server=adobe; the [A] async audio tools are honestly tagged and are in-scope for the Adobe x Claude product; the remaining steps are headless [C] asset upload/copy/folder ops. Slot-match verified: category audio_clean, operation_family Motion & Audio (O3), output_modality audio, workflow_nature=create, vertical arts_culture_music — a NEW audio deliverable (the mix-ready :30/:60 spot package) assembled from SUPPLIED parts, non-generatively; brand Foxglove Records is fictional and IP-clean.
